metadata.title: Why AI Produces Lesson Plans Where the Teacher Does All the Thinking
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut for claude-for-education-a-practitioners-guide candidate 08. Source chapter: claude-for-education-a-practitioners-guide/chapters/04-lesson-planning-and-activity-design.md (cognitive labor audit / receiving vs. producing; no full five-question audit; no backward design theory lecture; no timing section).
metadata.clock: narration (ElevenLabs) — durations below are word-count estimates until GATE 0 audio lock

style_bible.visual_style: editorial paper on newsprint; flat annotation plane; serif labels with hairline underlines
style_bible.color_palette: cream ground, charcoal serif type, TEAL = producing / student thinking / active, CRIMSON = receiving / teacher thinking / passive

metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = student producing / retrieving / applying / explaining; CRIMSON = student receiving / watching / copying / looking up; never swap mid-film
metadata.note: Card exclusions honored: NO full five-question audit enumeration; NO backward design theory in full; NO Freeman active learning meta-analysis detail; NO timing adjustment discussion; NO differentiation section. Core mechanism only: Claude models the lesson-plan genre — that genre centers the teacher; cognitive labor audit asks who is doing the thinking.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: Deshawn asks Claude for a lesson plan on chemical reactions. He gets a teacher-led demonstration, a worksheet with ten definitional questions, and a homework problem set. The plan is complete. It has a learning objective, materials, timing, and closure. Three weeks later, on the unit test, he learns what his students did not understand.
beats.card_text:
A lesson plan on chemical reactions.
Complete. Polished.
Timestamp: Tuesday morning.
beats.notes: CARD: the plan summary. Narration builds the problem — everything looked right.

beats.narration_text: Claude produced a plausible lesson. Deshawn accepted it because it looked right. That is the central risk of AI-assisted lesson planning: plausibility masquerading as alignment.
beats.notes: STILL: high school chemistry teacher at a desk reviewing a printed lesson plan, concerned, editorial desaturated style.

beats.narration_text: A complete lesson plan with objective, materials, timing, and closure should predict student cognitive engagement. Here is the case where it predicted passive compliance instead. Why?
beats.card_text:
A complete lesson plan should predict
student engagement.
Here it didn't. Why?
beats.notes: THE QUESTION beat — named on screen AND narration.

beats.narration_text: Claude models the lesson-plan genre. And that genre centers the teacher. The demonstration: teacher thinks, students watch. The worksheet: students look up definitions — no chemical reasoning required. The homework problem set: students practice, but all feedback is deferred three weeks to a grade. Every activity in this plan has the teacher doing the intellectual work.
beats.notes: Three activity rows. Each row: activity name on left, CRIMSON 'RECEIVING' tag on right. Arrows label each: 'teacher thinks' / 'look up answer' / 'feedback deferred'. Progressive reveal.

beats.narration_text: One audit question changes this: who is doing the thinking? Receiving: watching, listening, copying, looking up. Producing: retrieving, applying, explaining, predicting. Students learn by thinking, not by being told. A lesson that keeps all the thinking with the teacher is a lesson where students are present but not learning.
beats.notes: Two-column comparison. Left (CRIMSON): RECEIVING — watching / listening / copying / looking up. Right (TEAL): PRODUCING — retrieving / applying / explaining / predicting. Each word appears in sequence. TEAL label: 'who is doing the thinking?'

beats.narration_text: Deshawn audits his plan. Activity one: teacher demonstrates combustion reaction — receiving. Redesigned: students predict what products will form before the demo, then compare their prediction to observation — producing. Activity two: students copy the equation — receiving. Redesigned: students write the equation from their own prediction notes — producing. Activity three: worksheet asks 'what are the reactants?' — lookup, receiving. Redesigned: 'Why did we need oxygen? What would happen in a sealed container?' — explanation, producing.
beats.notes: Three rows, each showing a CRIMSON 'RECEIVING' activity that flips to a TEAL 'PRODUCING' redesign. Activity name on left. Tag on right flips from CRIMSON to TEAL. A short desc appears below the flip.

beats.narration_text: Claude is excellent at generating lesson plans where the teacher is active and the student is passive. It models the lesson-plan genre, and that genre often centers the teacher. Your audit task is to check, for every major activity, who is doing the thinking.
beats.card_text:
Claude models the lesson-plan genre.
That genre centers the teacher.
Your audit changes that.

beats.narration_text: One audit. For every major activity in the plan, write one word: receiving or producing. Then take one receiving moment and redesign it. Add one question students must answer from their own thinking — not from the board, not from a definition, not from looking at what the teacher just did.
beats.notes: Notepad card. Text writes in: the one-step audit. TEAL bracket: 'the audit'. Serif instruction below: 'one receiving moment → one producing redesign'.

beats.narration_text: Plausibility is not alignment. A plan looks right when it mirrors plans you've seen before. It is right when students are doing the thinking.
beats.card_text:
Plausibility is not alignment.
Students learn by thinking,
not by being told.
beats.notes: Recap endcard. AI IN EDUCATION topic kicker.
